differences.title: 1. ARCHITECTURE
differences.leftDesc: Hydrates only necessary components, boosting initial load performance for content-heavy sites.
differences.rightDesc: Flexible routing and rendering options for diverse application needs, including SSR and SSG.

differences.title: 2. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Achieves excellent performance by sending minimal or no JavaScript to the client by default.
differences.rightDesc: Offers powerful features but may require more client-side JavaScript for interactive UIs.

differences.title: 3. USE CASE
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for blogs, portfolios, documentation, and marketing websites where content is primary.
differences.rightDesc: Well-suited for complex applications, e-commerce platforms, and dynamic user dashboards.

differences.title: 4. RENDERING
differences.leftDesc: Primarily renders HTML on the server, sending minimal JS for interactivity on demand.
differences.rightDesc: Supports SSR, SSG, ISR, and Client-side rendering for optimized performance.

differences.title: 5. FRAMEWORK AGNOSTIC
differences.leftDesc: Supports popular UI frameworks like React, Vue, Svelte, and Solid, or no framework.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily built around React, leveraging its ecosystem and features.

differences.title: 6. DEPLOYMENT
differences.leftDesc: Easily deployable on static hosting platforms due to its default static output.
differences.rightDesc: Requires a Node.js runtime environment for server-side rendering.

differences.title: 7. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Simpler concepts focused on content delivery and progressive enhancement.
differences.rightDesc: Steeper curve due to advanced routing, rendering, and React concepts.
